content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents (from Topic A) can be framed as the active pruning and updating mechanism for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent long-term memory, where intrinsic curiosity signals double as a memory importance ranking that outperforms traditional recency or frequency-based filtering. This creates a self-sustaining learning loop where the agent’s inherent desire to explore unknown information automatically grows and refines its own memory store without manual intervention.
